subject: pickle deserialization
truth: Stop using pickle.loads, pickle.load, pickle.Unpickler, dill.loads, dill.load, dill.Unpickler, shelve.open, jsonpickle.decode, jsonpickle.unpickler.decode, jsonpickle.unpickler.Unpickler, or pandas.read_pickle on untrusted data; use a safe serialization format like JSON or a schema-validated parser instead.
quote: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

subject: Python random module for security purposes
truth: Stop using random.Random, random.random, random.randint, random.choice, random.choices, random.uniform, random.triangular, random.randbytes, random.sample, random.randrange, or random.getrandbits for security/cryptographic purposes; use the secrets module instead.
quote: Standard pseudo-random generators are not suitable for security/cryptographic purposes

subject: jsonpickle.decode
truth: Stop using jsonpickle.decode(), jsonpickle.unpickler.decode(), or jsonpickle.unpickler.Unpickler().restore() on untrusted data; jsonpickle is a wrapper around pickle and inherits its deserialization risks.
quote: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

subject: XML parsing in Python
truth: Stop using xml.etree.cElementTree, xml.etree.ElementTree, xml.sax, xml.dom.expatbuilder, xml.dom.minidom, and xml.dom.pulldom to parse untrusted XML; use defusedxml equivalents instead.
quote: Using various XLM methods to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable

subject: lxml.etree.GlobalParserTLS
truth: Stop using lxml.etree.GlobalParserTLS or lxml.etree.getDefaultParser without configuring a parser that disables external entities; they return parsers vulnerable to XXE by default.
quote: xml_bad_etree

subject: Trojan Source attack via invisible Unicode reordering
truth: Stop relying on visual code review to catch logic-altering Unicode characters; run Bandit rule B613 to detect bidirectional control characters that silently change program logic.
quote: A Python source file contains bidirectional control characters

subject: HTTPSConnection on old Python
truth: Stop using httplib.HTTPSConnection / http.client.HTTPSConnection on Python < 2.7.9 or < 3.4.3; upgrade to a modern Python version that provides certificate validation by default.
quote: Use of HTTPSConnection on older versions of Python prior to 2.7.9 and 3.4.3 do not provide security

subject: hashlib.new with weak hash algorithms
truth: Stop using hashlib.new('md5'), hashlib.new('md4'), hashlib.new('sha1'), or hashlib.new('sha') for security purposes; pass usedforsecurity=False or use a strong hash like sha256.
quote: Use of weak MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash for security. Consider usedforsecurity=False

subject: os.chmod with group-writable or group-executable permissions
truth: Stop using os.chmod with stat.S_IWGRP, stat.S_IXGRP, or masks like 0o664, 0o770, 0o776, 0o760 that grant group write/execute; restrict to owner-only or owner+group read-only.
quote: a file is set to group write or executable and a HIGH warning is reported if a

subject: unsafe package usage
truth: Stop using unsafe.Pointer, uintptr, unsafe.String, unsafe.Slice, and unsafe.StringData/unsafe.SliceData without explicit audit and safety review; use safe Go idioms instead.
quote: Audit the use of unsafe block

subject: Go HTTP client redirect policy
truth: Stop propagating sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, or all headers) across redirects; use a restrictive CheckRedirect that returns an error or only sets safe headers.
quote: Unsafe redirect policy may propagate sensitive headers

subject: Log injection via taint analysis (G706)
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input directly as the message argument to log/slog.Warn, log.Printf, or log.Println; pass tainted values as structured key-value pairs instead.
quote: tainted message - should be flagged
code_bad: slog.Warn(msg) // tainted message - should be flagged
code_good: slog.Warn("Error getting FS to serve", "ext", fileExtension, "path", r.URL.Path)

subject: os.chmod with numeric octal masks that are overly permissive
truth: Stop using os.chmod with numeric masks like 0o227, 0o7, 511 (0o777), or 0x1ff (0o777); use restrictive masks like 0o400, 0o600, or 0o644 for owner-only or owner+group read.
quote: This plugin test looks for the use of ``chmod`` and will alert when it is used

subject: Wildcard injection in subprocess/shell calls
truth: Stop passing bare '*' wildcards to shell commands like chown, chmod, tar, or rsync; use explicit file lists or escape the wildcard to prevent symlink/trojan-file attacks.
quote: Possible wildcard injection in call: subprocess.Popen

subject: Go HTTP server without timeouts
truth: Stop using http.ListenAndServe, http.ListenAndServeTLS, http.Serve, and http.ServeTLS without configuring ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on the http.Server.
quote: Use of net/http serve function that has no support for setting timeouts

subject: Log injection via taint analysis (G706)
truth: Stop using user-controlled strings as the format argument to log.Printf; use structured logging with key-value pairs or safe format verbs like %d after type conversion.
quote: log.Printf("User logged in: %s", username)

subject: Mako templates lack auto-escaping
truth: Stop using Mako templates without explicit escaping; use the 'h' (HTML escape), 'n' (no-op), or 'x' (XML) filter flags on every variable expression like ${ data |h }.
quote: Mako has no environment wide variable escaping mechanism

subject: Go range loop variable aliasing with nested struct field address
quote: Implicit memory aliasing in RangeStmt

associative: address of nested field in loop

_audit_fix: WRONG: code_bad and code_good are byte-for-byte identical text, so the shown 'fix' does not actually fix anything; the rule is also obsolete on Go 1.22+ where range-lo

subject: SQL query construction with % formatting
truth: Stop building SQL queries with string interpolation (% formatting); use parameterized queries with a tuple of parameters as the second argument to cursor.execute().
quote: Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction
code_bad: cur.execute("SELECT * FROM foo WHERE id = '%s'" % identifier)
code_good: cur.execute("SELECT * FROM foo WHERE id = '%s'", identifier)

subject: Go struct serialization leaks sensitive fields via default JSON marshaling
truth: Stop serializing structs with sensitive fields using json.Marshal/json.MarshalIndent/json.NewEncoder.Encode without tagging the field with `json:"-"` to exclude it.
quote: gosec rule G117

subject: Mako vs Jinja2 auto-escaping
truth: Stop assuming Mako auto-escapes like Jinja2; Mako has no environment-wide auto-escaping, so every variable must be explicitly filtered with 'h', 'n', or 'x' flags.
quote: Unlike Jinja2 (an alternative templating system), Mako has no environment wide variable escaping mechanism

subject: Go range loop variable aliasing with struct pointer slice
truth: Only take/avoid-taking the address of a range loop variable based on the module's Go version: for modules whose go.mod `go` directive is 1.21 or lower, the range variable (e.g. `sample`) is reused across iterations, so taking its address inside the loop body (or in a closure/goroutine) captures a single shared memory location and is a bug (gosec G601) — copy it to a local variable first (`sample := sample`) before taking its address. For modules with `go 1.22` or later in go.mod, Go's per-iteration loop-variable semantics (Go 1.22, Feb 2024) already give each iteration its own variable, so this pattern is no longer a bug and gosec v2.20.0+ itself skips/ignores the G601 rule for Go 1.22+ code — do not apply this fix (or flag this pattern) without first checking the go.mod `go` version.
quote: Implicit memory aliasing in RangeStmt
_audit_fix: STALE: This gosec G601 rule targets pre-Go-1.22 semantics; Go 1.22 (Feb 2024) changed range loops to give each iteration its own variable, so for any module with `go 1

subject: os.chmod with world-writable or world-executable permissions
truth: Stop using os.chmod with 0o777, 0o770, 0o776, 0o760, 0x1ff, or any mask granting world write/execute; restrict permissions to owner-only or owner+group read-only.
quote: Test for setting permissive file permissions

subject: Paramiko exec_command with string concatenation
truth: Stop building exec_command commands via string concatenation or f-strings with user input; use shlex.quote() or pass a list of arguments to avoid shell injection.
quote: These commands are run within a shell on the target and are thus vulnerable to various shell injection attacks.

subject: httpx.Client() without timeout
truth:
For httpx: only explicit `timeout=None` on httpx.Client()/AsyncClient() (or a per-request call) disables the timeout and risks an indefinite hang. httpx.Client()/AsyncClient() already default to a 5-second timeout (httpx.Timeout(timeout=5.0)) when the timeout parameter is simply omitted, so omission alone is safe. This differs from `requests.get()/post()`, which has NO default timeout at all — omitting `timeout` there causes indefinite hangs and should always be flagged.</corrected_fix>
</invoke>
quote: Call to requests without timeout
_audit_fix: STALE: httpx.Client()/AsyncClient() ship with a default 5-second timeout, so simply omitting the timeout parameter does NOT hang indefinitely — that behavior is specif

subject: Using fmt.Sprintf for SQL query construction
truth: Stop using fmt.Sprintf to build SQL query strings with any user-controlled input; use database/sql parameter placeholders (? or $1) and pass values as arguments.
quote: Format string without proper quoting
code_bad:
q := fmt.Sprintf("select * from foo where name = '%s'", os.Args[1])
rows, err := db.Query(q)

subject: Binding to all interfaces with 0.0.0.0
truth: Stop binding to '0.0.0.0' which opens the service on all network interfaces; bind to a specific IP address like '127.0.0.1' or a documented internal IP instead.
quote: Binding to all network interfaces can potentially open up a service to traffic on unintended interfaces

subject: tarfile.extractall() without members or filter
truth: Stop using tarfile.extractall() without a members filter or filter parameter; pass members=members_filter(tar) or filter='data' to prevent directory traversal.
quote: * ``tarfile.extractall()`` - members from the archive is trusted - HIGH

subject: tarfile.extractall() with filter='fully_trusted'
truth: Stop using filter='fully_trusted' on tarfile.extractall() unless you have verified the archive source; use filter='data' to strip dangerous members by default.
quote: def filter_fully_trusted_archive_handler(filename):

subject: Function argument as HTTP request URL
truth: Stop passing a URL as a function argument to http.Get() unless the caller only supplies constant values; a function argument is not considered a secure source.
quote: An url provided as a function argument is not secure
code_bad:
func get(url string) {
	resp, err := http.Get(url)

subject: TLS VerifyPeerCertificate without VerifyConnection
truth: Stop setting VerifyPeerCertificate without also setting VerifyConnection; TLS session resumption bypasses VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset
code_good:
VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil },
		VerifyConnection:      func(_ tls.ConnectionState) error { return nil }

subject: Go gosec G112 suppression comments
truth: Suppress G112 with // #nosec G112 or //gosec:disable G112 only when the server is not publicly exposed; otherwise the suppression hides a real vulnerability.
quote: #nosec G112 - Not publicly exposed

subject: GetConfigForClient returning VerifyPeerCertificate without VerifyConnection
truth: When returning a *tls.Config from GetConfigForClient that sets VerifyPeerCertificate, also set VerifyConnection or disable session tickets to prevent bypass.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset
code_good:
VerifyPeerCertificate: func(_ [][]byte, _ [][]*x509.Certificate) error { return nil },
		VerifyConnection:      func(_ tls.ConnectionState) error { return nil }

subject: Missing symlink safety check in zip extraction
truth: Stop extracting symlinks from archives without verifying the target path; check that the resolved symlink destination stays within the extraction directory.
quote: if f.FileInfo().Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 { return nil }

subject: Unicode bidirectional control characters in source code
truth: Stop embedding Unicode bidirectional control characters (U+202E, U+2066, U+2069) in Python source files; use a linter like Bandit rule B613 to detect them.
quote: Those characters can be embedded in comments and strings to reorder source code characters in a way that changes its logic.

subject: Hugging Face Hub downloads without revision pinning
truth: Always pin to a specific immutable commit hash when downloading from Hugging Face Hub, never rely on mutable tags or branch names like 'main' or 'v1.0.0'.
quote: Downloading models, datasets, or files without specifying a revision based on an immmutable revision (commit) can lead to supply chain attacks

subject: Suppressing G101 with #nosec annotation
truth: Use #nosec G101 annotation only for false-positive config keys, never for actual secrets; the annotation must appear on the same line as the declaration.
quote: // #nosec G101

subject: Flask debug mode
truth: Stop using debug=True for convenience in development; use environment variables or config files to control debug mode and never let it reach production.
quote: Running Flask applications in debug mode results in the Werkzeug debugger being enabled

subject: variable-based header assignment
truth: Even when assigning headers via a variable reference, setting both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length on the same response creates a smuggling vector.
quote: conflicting headers or bare LF in body parsing

subject: lxml.etree.RestrictedElement
truth: Stop using lxml.etree.RestrictedElement without understanding its security implications; it does not fully protect against XML external entity attacks.
quote: xml_bad_etree

subject: Package-level URL variable in HTTP request
truth: Stop declaring the URL for http.Get() as a package-level variable; declare it as a const or a function-scope variable initialized to a constant string.
quote: // Variable defined a package level can be changed at any time

subject: ssh.PublicKeyCallback returning nil error without validation
truth: Always validate the public key inside PublicKeyCallback and return nil error only if authorized; returning nil unconditionally bypasses authentication.
quote: Stateful misuse of ssh.PublicKeyCallback leading to auth bypass

subject: Hardcoded credentials in Go source code
truth: Stop hardcoding passwords, tokens, or secrets as string literals in Go source code; use environment variables, secret stores, or config files instead.
quote: gosec rule G101

subject: lxml.etree.parse
truth: Bandit B320 flags lxml.etree.parse() and lxml.etree.fromstring() equally on untrusted XML — the entry-point function does not matter for XXE, since both accept the same parser= argument. Fix: construct an lxml.etree.XMLParser with resolve_entities=False and no_network=True and pass it to whichever of parse()/fromstring() you call, or use defusedxml's equivalent function instead. (Note: lxml 5.x+ disables external-entity expansion by default, but explicit resolve_entities=False/no_network, or defusedxml, is still the recommended defense-in-depth for untrusted input and for code that may run on older lxml.)
quote: xml_bad_etree
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: Bandit B320's actual fix is 'use the defusedxml equivalent function' — it does not distinguish parse() vs fromstring(); both accept the same lxml parser= argume

subject: aliased math/rand alongside crypto/rand
truth: Stop using math/rand (even aliased as mrand) alongside crypto/rand; gosec flags any use of math/rand or math/rand/v2 for security-sensitive contexts.
quote: Insecure random number source (rand)

subject: requests.get() with timeout=None
truth: Setting timeout=None is equivalent to omitting timeout entirely and will still cause the program to hang indefinitely; always set a numeric timeout.
quote: Call to requests with timeout set to None

subject: io.Copy zip extraction unbounded
truth: Stop using io.Copy when extracting zip file entries from untrusted archives; use io.CopyN with a size limit to prevent zip bomb decompression bombs.
quote: Detect io.Copy instead of io.CopyN when decompressing

subject: ssh.PublicKeyCallback stateful closure
truth: Do not store the key argument from PublicKeyCallback in a closure or package-level variable; always validate the key inline and return immediately.
quote: Stateful misuse of ssh.PublicKeyCallback leading to auth bypass

subject: Path traversal via user-controlled file paths
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input directly to file operations; sanitize the path with filepath.Clean, path.Base, or a whitelist of allowed paths.
quote: gosec rule G703: Path traversal via taint analysis

subject: SQL injection via fmt.Sprintf in QueryContext/Query
truth: Stop passing fmt.Sprintf results to db.Query, db.QueryContext, tx.QueryContext, or conn.QueryContext; use parameterized queries with placeholders.
quote: Format string without proper quoting with context
code_bad:
q := fmt.Sprintf("select * from foo where name = '%s'", os.Args[1])
rows, err := db.QueryContext(context.Background(), q)

subject: marshal file deserialization
truth: Stop using marshal.load to read serialized data from files; use a safe serialization format like JSON or a structured validation library instead.
quote: Deserialization with the marshal module is possibly dangerous.

subject: hashlib.new with usedforsecurity=True on weak algorithms
truth: Do not set usedforsecurity=False on hashlib.new/md5/sha1/md4/sha calls unless the hash is provably non-security (e.g. checksums, cache keys, dedup) — usedforsecurity=True is already the default and passing it explicitly changes nothing. Setting usedforsecurity=False bypasses FIPS-mode restrictions and silences Bandit's B324 'insecure hash' warning without fixing anything; if the hash actually protects passwords, tokens, or signatures, replace the weak algorithm with a strong one (SHA-256 for integrity/non-password use, or Argon2/bcrypt/scrypt for password hashing) instead of suppressing the warning.
quote: Use of weak MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash for security. Consider usedforsecurity=False
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: usedforsecurity=True is already hashlib's default (nobody 'enables' anything by passing it), and the fact omits the crucial caveat that usedforsecurity=False is

subject: Paramiko host key verification default
truth: Stop disabling host key verification; paramiko verifies host keys by default and you must not override that with AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy.
quote: host keys are verified by default

subject: ParseMultipartForm in helper functions
truth: Stop calling r.ParseMultipartForm in helper/utility functions that receive the request; apply MaxBytesReader at the handler entry point instead.
quote: Unbounded ParseMultipartForm in HTTP handlers can cause memory exhaustion

subject: exec.Command with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input (os.Args, os.Getenv) as the command name or arguments to exec.Command; use hardcoded command strings instead.
quote: // Calling any function which starts a new process with using

subject: Wildcard injection with os.system
truth: Stop passing bare '*' wildcards to os.system calls; use subprocess with argument lists or glob with explicit paths to avoid wildcard injection.
quote: Vulnerable to wildcard injection

subject: Jinja2 select_autoescape with no arguments
truth: select_autoescape() called with no arguments is already safe by default: enabled_extensions defaults to ('html','htm','xml') and default_for_string=True, so autoescaping is enabled for those template extensions (and for string-loaded templates) out of the box. Only pass an explicit extensions list if your templates use different/additional extensions that need autoescaping (e.g. .jinja, .j2). The actually insecure patterns are `autoescape=False` or omitting the autoescape setting entirely when constructing the Environment/Loader without select_autoescape — that is what Bandit B701 flags.
quote: select_autoescape(['html', 'htm', 'xml'])
_audit_fix: WRONG: Jinja2's select_autoescape() has default parameter enabled_extensions=('html','htm','xml') (confirmed on the current Jinja docs), so calling it with no argument

subject: markupsafe.Markup with dynamic content
truth: Stop passing dynamic/untrusted content directly to markupsafe.Markup; interpolate the resulting Markup object or use markupsafe.escape instead.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in http.NewRequestWithContext
truth: Stop passing user-controlled or env-var URLs to http.NewRequestWithContext; use a hardcoded path or validate the full URL against an allowlist.
quote:
target := os.Getenv("IMPORT_URL")
	ctx := context.Background()
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, target, http.NoBody)

subject: Paramiko exec_command shell injection
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to Paramiko's exec_command; use shlex.quote() or pass commands as argument lists to avoid shell injection.
quote: Possible shell injection via Paramiko call, check inputs are properly sanitized.

subject: ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey
truth: Stop using ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey which disables host key verification; use ssh.FixedHostKey or implement a proper HostKeyCallback instead.
quote: Audit the use of ssh.InsecureIgnoreHostKey function

subject: typed except with pass
truth: Stop silently passing on typed exceptions like ZeroDivisionError; log the error or handle it, as silent suppression hides operational issues.
quote: suppress errors

subject: Django mark_safe with try/except flow
truth: Stop calling mark_safe() on variables that may have been assigned in exception handlers or else/finally blocks where user data could flow in.
quote: safestring.mark_safe(my_insecure_str)

subject: filepath.WalkDir callback with os.Remove(path)
truth: Stop using the WalkDir callback path argument directly in os.Remove; use os.OpenRoot to get a root handle and call root.Remove(path) instead.
quote: Filesystem TOCTOU race risk in filepath.Walk/WalkDir callbacks

associative: remove all files in directory walk

subject: Overflow check via sign comparison after int16 cast
truth: Stop checking int16(bigValue) < 0 after casting an Atoi result to detect overflow; use strconv.ParseInt with bitSize 16 and check the error.
quote: if int16(bigValue) < 0
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: Same pattern as the other gosec-g109 fact in this batch: code_good just removes the overflow check entirely (`fmt.Println(bigValue)`) instead of showing the pre

subject: Zip archive extraction without path traversal protection
truth: Stop using file.Name directly in filepath.Join; validate that the resolved path stays within the target directory using a path prefix check.
quote: File path traversal when extracting zip archive

subject: CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input from query parameters directly to AddInsecureBypassPattern; use a fixed allowlist of safe path prefixes.
quote: Unsafe CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns

subject: Hugging Face snapshot_download without commit pinning
truth: Always pin snapshot_download to an immutable commit hash revision, never use mutable branch names like 'main' or PR refs like 'refs/pr/1'.
quote: snapshot_download(repo_id="org/model_name", revision="main")

subject: markupsafe.Markup with percent-format strings
truth: Stop passing percent-formatted strings with dynamic content to markupsafe.Markup; use Markup.format() on an escaped Markup object instead.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities

subject: PyTorch torch.load
truth: PyTorch >=2.6 (Jan 2025+) already defaults torch.load(...) to weights_only=True, so a bare torch.load(f) call is safe by default on current PyTorch. Explicit weights_only=True (or safetensors) is still recommended as defense-in-depth and is REQUIRED hardening on pre-2.6 PyTorch or any pinned/legacy environment. The more important CURRENT landmine to flag: code that explicitly passes weights_only=False — a common workaround developers adopted to fix the BC-breaking checkpoint-loading errors the 2.6 default flip caused — which knowingly re-opens the arbitrary-code-execution path and should be treated as the real red flag, not mere omission of the parameter.
quote: Using `torch.load` or `torch.serialization.load` with untrusted data can lead to arbitrary code execution.
_audit_fix: STALE: As of PyTorch 2.6 (released 2025), torch.load's weights_only default was flipped from False to True, so on current PyTorch the described insecure default no lon

subject: strconv.Atoi result cast to int32/int16
truth: Stop casting strconv.Atoi results to int32 or int16 without overflow checking; use strconv.ParseInt with the appropriate bit size instead.
quote: Converting strconv.Atoi result to int32/int16
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The code_good example is just `fmt.Println(bigValue)` — it never demonstrates strconv.ParseInt with a bit size as the truth prescribes, so a model shown this pa

subject: Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length ordering
truth: Do not set both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length on a response regardless of header order; pick exactly one transfer-coding mechanism.
quote: conflicting headers

associative: set content length then transfer encoding

subject: File path from tainted input (env var, query param, stdin)
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user/taint input directly to file I/O functions; sanitize the path with filepath.Clean() or filepath.Rel() first.
quote: File path provided as taint input

subject: XSS via unescaped user input in HTTP response
truth: Stop writing user-controlled input directly into HTTP responses without escaping HTML; use html.EscapeString() or equivalent sanitization.
quote: XSS via taint analysis

subject: markupsafe.Markup with direct variable
truth: Stop passing untrusted variables directly to markupsafe.Markup(); use markupsafe.escape() or interpolate into an existing Markup object.
quote: ``markupsafe.Markup`` does not perform any escaping, so passing dynamic content, like f-strings, variables or interpolated strings will potentially lead to XSS vulnerabilities

subject: Paramiko SSH host key verification
truth: Stop using AutoAddPolicy or WarningPolicy; set_missing_host_key_policy must use RejectPolicy or a custom policy that verifies host keys.
quote: Paramiko call with policy set to automatically trust the unknown host key

subject: Using path.Join instead of filepath.Join for archive extraction
truth: Stop using path.Join for archive extraction paths; use filepath.Join and then validate the resolved path is within the target directory.
quote: filePath := path.Join(destPath, f.Name)

subject: XSS via unescaped form value written directly to response
truth: Stop writing r.FormValue() data directly to the response writer without escaping; use html.EscapeString or structured output like JSON.
quote: XSS via taint analysis

subject: Mako template variable escaping
truth: Stop rendering Mako template variables without a filter; apply the 'h' filter for HTML context like ${ user_input |h } to prevent XSS.
quote: all input variables must be carefully escaped before use to prevent possible vulnerabilities to Cross Site Scripting (XSS) attacks

subject: Python 2 vs Python 3 input()
truth: Stop assuming input() is safe; in Python 2 it evaluates input as code — use raw_input() for Python 2 and only use input() in Python 3.
quote: On Python 2, use raw_input instead, input is safe in Python 3.

subject: CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns
truth: Stop using wildcard patterns like "/" or "/*" in AddInsecureBypassPattern; use explicit, narrow path prefixes like "/healthz" instead.
quote: Unsafe CrossOriginProtection bypass patterns

subject: text/template with user input
truth: Stop using text/template with user-controlled template strings or data; use html/template or escape user input with html.EscapeString.
quote: Server-side template injection via text/template

subject: Go HTTP server ReadHeaderTimeout
truth: Set ReadHeaderTimeout on http.Server to prevent slow-header attacks; omitting it leaves the server vulnerable to resource exhaustion.
quote: Detect ReadHeaderTimeout not configured as a potential risk

subject: File path from HTTP request query parameter
truth:
Stop treating filepath.Clean() as a fix for path traversal from r.URL.Query().Get() values passed to os.Open()/os.OpenFile(): Clean() only collapses redundant separators and resolves ".." lexically against a real preceding segment — it does NOT clamp the result to a base directory, so inputs like "../../etc/passwd" or an absolute path pass through unchanged and the file still opens outside the intended directory (this exact mistake caused a real CVE in GitLab's devfile parser, CVE-2023-49569). Instead: join the input into a fixed base directory (full := filepath.Join(baseDir, filepath.Clean("/"+userInput))) and verify the resulting path still has baseDir as a prefix before opening — or, on Go 1.24+, use the os.Root API (root, _ := os.OpenRoot(baseDir); root.Open(userInput)), which is purpose-built to be traversal-resistant and should be preferred over manual Clean()+prefix-check logic.</corrected_fix>
</invoke>
quote: File path provided as taint input
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: filepath.Clean() only normalizes redundant separators and '.'/'..' segments — it does not clamp a path to a base directory, so a value like "../../etc/passwd" (

subject: Django mark_safe with user-controlled input
truth: Stop passing user-controlled or dynamically-constructed strings to mark_safe(); only mark_safe() hardcoded, trusted string literals.
quote: Use of mark_safe() may expose cross-site scripting vulnerabilities

subject: ssl._create_unverified_context
truth: Stop using ssl._create_unverified_context; use ssl.create_default_context instead to get certificate validation and hostname checks.
quote: behavior that does not validate certificates or perform hostname checks.

subject: Hardcoded IV/nonce in AES encryption
truth: Stop using a static, hardcoded IV/nonce for AES encryption; generate a random nonce using crypto/rand for each encryption operation.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_good:
iv := make([]byte, 16)
rand.Read(iv)
block, _ := aes.NewCipher([]byte("12345678123456781234567812345678"))
_ = cipher.NewCTR(block, iv)

subject: Open redirect via user-controlled URL parameter
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to http.Redirect; validate the redirect target against an allowlist or use a fixed path.
quote: Open redirect via taint analysis
code_bad:
target := r.URL.Query().Get("next")
	http.Redirect(w, r, target, http.StatusFound)

subject: logging.config.listen
truth: Stop using logging.config.listen() which passes configuration through eval(); use a secure configuration loading mechanism instead.
quote: portions of the configuration are passed through eval(), use of this function may open its users to a security risk

subject: XML parsing with ElementTree
truth: Stop using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse, iterparse, fromstring, and XMLParser on untrusted data; use defusedxml.ElementTree instead. (Bandit B314 "xml_bad_ElementTree" — not B313, which covers xml.etree.cElementTree.) Update source/lib metadata from "Bandit B313"/"bandit-b313" to "Bandit B314"/"bandit-b314".
quote: Methods should be replaced with their defusedxml equivalents.
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The fact mislabels the Bandit rule: B313 is 'xml_bad_cElementTree' (targets xml.etree.cElementTree), while the methods listed (xml.etree.ElementTree.parse/iterp

subject: torch.load weights_only=False
truth: Stop using torch.load with weights_only=False; this explicitly enables unsafe pickle deserialization and arbitrary code execution.
quote: Example of loading with weights_only=False (should trigger B614)

subject: Hardcoded credentials in const/var declarations
truth: Stop declaring passwords or secrets as package-level var or const string literals; use a secrets manager or environment injection.
quote: gosec rule G101

subject: tarfile member directory traversal validation
truth: When using members=, validate each member by checking for '../' in member.name and symlink/hardlink traversal in member.linkname.
quote: Discard files that contain a directory

subject: Django mark_safe with format() and user data
truth: Stop using mark_safe() with .format() on strings that contain any user-controlled variables; use only hardcoded format arguments.
quote: safestring.mark_safe('{} {}'.format(my_insecure_str, 'STR'))

subject: Serving filesystem root via http.FileServer
truth: Stop serving the entire filesystem root with http.FileServer(http.Dir("/")); restrict to a specific subdirectory like "./public".
quote: http.FileServer(http.Dir("/"))

subject: TLS session resumption bypasses VerifyPeerCertificate
truth: Disable session tickets (SessionTicketsDisabled = true) when using VerifyPeerCertificate alone, or pair it with VerifyConnection.
quote: TLS resumption may bypass VerifyPeerCertificate when VerifyConnection is unset

subject: context.WithTimeout without defer cancel()
truth: Always call the cancel function returned by context.WithTimeout/WithCancel/WithDeadline via defer cancel() to release resources.
quote:
child, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
	defer cancel()
code_good:
child, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)
	defer cancel()

subject: Path concatenation with tainted input
truth: Stop concatenating tainted input with a directory prefix. filepath.Join() already calls Clean() internally, so Join()+Clean() together still just lexically normalize the path — they do not enforce that the result stays inside the base directory. filepath.Join(dir, "../../../etc/passwd") cleans straight to a path outside dir. After joining/cleaning, explicitly verify containment before opening the file, e.g.: cleanPath := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(dir, filePath)); if !strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, dir+string(os.PathSeparator)) { reject }. (Prefer comparing absolute paths, and note the HasPrefix check itself needs dir to end without a trailing separator mismatch / or use filepath.Rel and check the result doesn't start with "..".)
quote: File path provided as taint input
_audit_fix: WRONG: code_good (filepath.Join(dir, filePath) then filepath.Clean) does not actually stop path traversal: Join already Cleans internally, and a filePath like '../../e

subject: Hardcoded credentials in const blocks
truth: Stop embedding secrets in const blocks; gosec G101 flags any string literal matching entropy patterns for passwords or tokens.
quote: gosec rule G101

subject: ParseMultipartForm with MaxBytesReader but no error check
truth: Stop ignoring the error from r.ParseMultipartForm even when MaxBytesReader is set; the error signals truncation or rejection.
quote: Unbounded ParseMultipartForm in HTTP handlers can cause memory exhaustion
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: code_good does not demonstrate the claimed fix at all: it replaces r.ParseMultipartForm(32<<20) with r.ParseForm() — a different function that doesn't parse mul

subject: Go range loop variable aliasing
truth: gosec G601 (implicit memory aliasing of range loop variables) applies only to modules whose go.mod declares a Go version below 1.22. In those pre-1.22 modules, don't take the address of (or capture in a closure) the range loop variable 'item' directly inside the loop — assign it to a local variable first or index into the slice/map instead. In go.mod 1.22+ modules this is no longer a bug: Go 1.22 gives range/for loop variables fresh per-iteration scope, and gosec itself (v2.20.0+) skips/ignores the G601 check for 1.22+ modules, so the pattern is safe and won't be flagged.
quote: Implicit memory aliasing in RangeStmt
_audit_fix: STALE: Since Go 1.22, for/range loop variables have per-iteration scope by default, which eliminates the implicit-aliasing bug G601 detects for any module declaring `g

subject: SMTP command/header injection via taint analysis
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input directly to smtp.SendMail or smtp.Client.Mail/Rcpt; parse addresses with net/mail first.
quote: SMTP command/header injection via taint analysis

subject: typed except with continue in loop
truth: Even typed 'except SomeError: continue' is bad practice unless explicitly configured; prefer logging or handling the error.
quote: This pattern is considered bad practice in general, but also represents a potential security issue

subject: Integer type conversion without overflow check
truth: Stop converting a larger integer type to a smaller type without first checking the value is within the target type's range.
quote: Type conversion which leads to integer overflow
code_bad:
var a uint32 = math.MaxUint32
b := int32(a)
code_good:
var a uint16 = math.MaxUint16
b := int32(a)

subject: Overflow in arithmetic before type conversion
quote: return uint8(x * 10) // [10, 290] -> overflows uint8
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: The code_good 'addProp' function still computes x+10 in uint8 arithmetic before widening to uint16 (Go promotes the untyped constant 10 to match x's uint8 type)

subject: httpx.Client with verify=False
truth: Stop passing verify=False to httpx.Client or httpx.AsyncClient; always pass verify=True to enforce certificate validation.
quote: Call to requests with verify=False disabling SSL certificate checks, security issue.

subject: HTTP response header conflict
truth: Never set both Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length headers on the same response; doing so enables HTTP request smuggling.
quote: HTTP request smuggling via conflicting headers or bare LF in body parsing

subject: hashlib.new with uppercase weak algorithm names
truth: Stop using hashlib.new with uppercase variants like 'MD4', 'SHA', or 'SHA1'; they are still insecure and will be flagged.
quote: Use of weak MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash for security

subject: os.Args used directly in file operations
truth: Stop using os.Args (or any external input) directly in file operations, and do not rely on filepath.Abs() to make that safe — Abs only normalizes a path to absolute form (via Clean) and does not stop directory traversal; a caller can still supply '../' segments or an absolute path that resolves outside the intended directory. Validate before opening: filepath.Clean the input, then confirm the cleaned result still starts with (strings.HasPrefix) a fixed base directory, or better, map the untrusted argument through an allowlist / numeric-ID-to-file table rather than opening a caller-supplied path at all.
quote: os.Open(os.Args[1])
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: code_good doesn't validate or sanitize anything: it reads an arbitrary path from os.Getenv("FILE") and calls filepath.Abs on it, which does not restrict path tr

subject: Path traversal via taint analysis in Go
truth: Stop using unsanitized user input as file paths; use filepath.Clean, path.Base, or numeric ID mapping to restrict access.
quote: gosec rule G703: Path traversal via taint analysis
code_good:
id := r.FormValue("id")
num, _ := strconv.Atoi(id)
os.Open("/tmp/file" + strconv.Itoa(num))

subject: Python assert removed under -O flag
truth: Stop using assert for runtime security checks; they are silently removed when Python runs with -O (optimized byte code).
quote: assert is removed with compiling to optimised byte code

subject: Paramiko AutoAddPolicy and WarningPolicy
truth: Stop using AutoAddPolicy and WarningPolicy; they silently accept unknown host keys, defeating SSH identity verification.
quote: automatically trust the unknown host key

subject: SSRF via user-controlled URL in http.Get
truth: Stop passing user-controlled input directly to http.Get; validate and restrict URLs to an allowlist of known-safe hosts.
quote:
url := r.URL.Query().Get("url")
	http.Get(url)

subject: Open redirect via concatenated user input
truth: Stop concatenating user input into a redirect URL; validate or sanitize the destination before passing to http.Redirect.
quote: Open redirect via taint analysis

subject: Path traversal via r.URL.Path in HTTP handler
truth: Stop passing http.Dir("/") (the filesystem root) to http.FileServer / as the base directory for HTTP file serving; gosec G111 flags exactly the literal source construct http.Dir("/") (or http.Dir('/')) because it exposes the entire filesystem to path traversal via crafted request paths — it does not flag generic unsanitized use of r.URL.Path elsewhere in a response. Serve from a restricted subdirectory (e.g. http.Dir("./static")) instead, and sanitize any path segments derived from r.URL.Path separately if they are used to build filesystem paths.
quote: r.URL.Path[1:]
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: Misattributed to gosec rule G111: G111's actual (and only) detection pattern is the literal source construct http.Dir("/") (root-directory file serving), not ge

subject: Hardcoded nonce in AES-GCM
truth: Stop using a static nonce with AES-GCM; extract the nonce from the ciphertext or generate it randomly with crypto/rand.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_good:
nonce, ciphertext := data[:nonceSize], data[nonceSize:]
return gcm.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, nil)

subject: Hardcoded byte-slice IV in AES-CTR/OFB
truth: Stop using a literal byte-slice as the IV for cipher.NewCTR or cipher.NewOFB; generate the IV from crypto/rand instead.
quote: Use of hardcoded IV/nonce for encryption
code_good:
iv := make([]byte, 16)
rand.Read(iv)
block, _ := aes.NewCipher([]byte("12345678123456781234567812345678"))
_ = cipher.NewCTR(block, iv)

subject: Jinja2 autoescaping
truth: Stop relying on Jinja2's default autoescape=False; always set autoescape=True or use select_autoescape to prevent XSS.
quote: autoescaping is False by default

subject: Go HTTP server ReadTimeout vs ReadHeaderTimeout
truth: Set either ReadHeaderTimeout or ReadTimeout (or both) on http.Server to satisfy gosec G112 and mitigate Slowloris attacks. Since gosec PR #825 (merged June 2022), G112 no longer requires ReadHeaderTimeout specifically — setting ReadTimeout alone is sufficient, matching Go's documented fallback (ReadHeaderTimeout defaults to ReadTimeout's value when zero). Prefer setting ReadHeaderTimeout explicitly for clarity/tighter header-phase timeout, but it is not strictly required by current gosec to avoid the G112 warning.
quote: Detect ReadHeaderTimeout not configured as a potential risk
_audit_fix: STALE: This was only true in gosec's original buggy G112 implementation; gosec PR #825 ('Fix ReadTimeout for G112', merged June 2022) changed the rule so that setting

subject: exec.Command with environment variable in command name
truth: Stop concatenating environment variables into the command name passed to exec.Command; use a hardcoded command string.
quote: // variable is consider as a dangerous user input

subject: ParseMultipartForm without MaxBytesReader
truth: Stop calling r.ParseMultipartForm without first wrapping r.Body with http.MaxBytesReader to limit memory consumption.
quote: Unbounded ParseMultipartForm in HTTP handlers can cause memory exhaustion

subject: Python assert for interface constraints
truth: Stop using assert to enforce interface constraints; raise a semantically meaningful error or AssertionError instead.
quote: assert is removed with compiling to optimised byte code

subject: bare except with pass
truth: Stop catching exceptions with bare 'except:' and silently passing; at minimum log the error or handle it explicitly.
quote: catch an exception and silently ignore it

subject: pandas.read_pickle
truth: Stop using pandas.read_pickle() on untrusted data; it deserializes pickled objects which can execute arbitrary code.
quote: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

subject: Go HTTP client Authorization header on redirect
truth: Stop setting Authorization header on redirect requests; the idiomatic pattern leaks bearer tokens to external hosts.
quote: Unsafe redirect policy may propagate sensitive headers

subject: eval() vs ast.literal_eval() in Python
truth: Stop reaching for eval() to parse literals; use ast.literal_eval() which safely evaluates only literal expressions.
quote: consider using safer ast.literal_eval

subject: Narrowing conversion without range check
truth: Always validate the value is within [math.MinIntN, math.MaxIntN] or [0, math.MaxUintN] before narrowing conversion.
quote: if a < math.MinInt32 || a > math.MaxInt32 { panic("out of range") }
code_bad:
var a uint = math.MaxUint
b := int16(a)

subject: SSRF via environment variable URL in http.Post
truth: Stop passing environment-variable URLs directly to http.Post; hardcode or validate the target against an allowlist.
quote:
target := os.Getenv("TARGET_URL")
	http.Post(target, "text/plain", nil)

subject: Python exec()
truth: Stop using exec() with untrusted input — it enables arbitrary Python code execution (eval/code injection, CWE-95), a distinct vulnerability class from OS command injection (CWE-78, which applies to os.system()/subprocess-style shell command construction). Mitigation is to avoid exec()/eval() on any externally influenced data entirely, not to shell-escape.
quote: Use of exec detected
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: exec() runs arbitrary Python code (code/eval injection), not an OS shell command; conflating it with 'OS command injection' (the class for os.system/subprocess

subject: SQL query construction with str.replace
truth: Stop building SQL queries with str.replace(); use parameterized queries with placeholder values passed separately.
quote: Flag when str.replace is used in the string construction
_audit_fix: WRONG: The code_good example is itself broken as a parameterized query: it quotes the placeholder as `'%s'` (placeholders should not be manually quoted — the driver ha

subject: except Exception with continue in loop
truth: Stop using 'except Exception: continue' in loops; errors should at least be logged as they may indicate an attack.
quote: A larger than normal volume of errors from a service can indicate an attempt is being made to disrupt or interfere with it

subject: unsafe.String usage
truth: Stop using unsafe.String to create strings from arbitrary byte pointers; use string(byteSlice) conversion instead.
quote: Audit the use of unsafe block

subject: seeded math/rand generator via rand.New
truth: Stop using rand.New with math/rand or math/rand/v2 for security-sensitive random numbers; use crypto/rand instead.
quote: Insecure random number source (rand)

subject: Hardcoded temp file paths
truth: Stop using hardcoded paths like /tmp, /var/tmp, or /dev/shm for temporary files; use the tempfile module instead.
quote: Probable insecure usage of temp file/directory

subject: except Exception with pass
truth: Stop catching 'Exception' broadly and silently passing; log the error or handle specific exception types instead.
quote: catch an exception and silently ignore it

subject: SMTP command injection via smtp.Client.Mail/Rcpt
truth: Stop passing raw query/form values to smtp.Client.Mail and Rcpt; validate addresses with mail.ParseAddress first.
quote: SMTP command/header injection via taint analysis
code_bad:
from := r.URL.Query().Get("from")
to := r.URL.Query().Get("to")
_ = c.Mail(from)
_ = c.Rcpt(to)

subject: filepath.Walk callback with os.Remove(path)
truth: Stop using the Walk callback path argument directly in os.Remove; hardcode a known-safe path or use os.OpenRoot.
quote: Filesystem TOCTOU race risk in filepath.Walk/WalkDir callbacks

subject: torch.load with map_location
truth: Stop using torch.load with only map_location; always add weights_only=True to prevent arbitrary code execution.
quote: Example of loading with map_location but no weights_only (should trigger B614)

subject: Environment variable as HTTP request URL
truth: Stop using os.Getenv() to supply a URL to http.Get(); environment variables are not considered a secure source.
quote: Environmental variables are not considered as secure source

subject: SQL query construction using fmt.Sprintf with user input
truth: Stop building SQL queries with fmt.Sprintf and user input; use parameterized queries with placeholders instead.
quote: SQL query construction using format string
code_bad:
q := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM foo where name = '%s'", os.Args[1])
rows, err := db.Query(q)

subject: Creating tempfile using a predictable path
truth: Stop using predictable paths like /tmp/demo2 for temporary files; use ioutil.TempFile or os.CreateTemp instead.
quote: Creating tempfile using a predictable path

subject: Go slice bounds out of range
truth: Always check that the slice index or slice range is within the actual length of the slice before accessing it.
quote: Possible slice bounds out of range

subject: os.chmod with world-executable permissions (S_IXOTH)
truth: Stop using os.chmod with stat.S_IXOTH or any mask granting world execute; restrict permissions to owner-only.
quote: a HIGH warning is reported if a file is set world write or executable

subject: template.HTML() with variable input
truth: Stop passing untrusted variables to template.HTML(); only use template.HTML() with hardcoded string literals.
quote: Using a variable to initialize could potentially be dangerous

subject: http.ServeFile with user-controlled path
truth: Stop passing user input directly to http.ServeFile or http.ServeFileFS; sanitize the path or use a whitelist.
quote: http.ServeFile(w, r, path)

subject: Command injection via user-controlled shell execution
truth: Remove sast-0cdb81ac51 as a duplicate of sast-a236cd645b (both describe the identical exec.Command("sh","-c", userInput) command-injection pattern under gosec G702). If a distinct fact is wanted instead of deletion, retag source to "gosec G702" (CWE-78, command injection) and drop the current quote field ("XSS via taint analysis" is G705's description, not G702's) -- replace it with an actual verbatim string from gosec's G702 rule description/source ("Command injection via taint analysis") rather than G705's. Do not use this id/slot to cover real G705 (XSS via taint analysis / CWE-79) -- that would need a brand-new fact with XSS-appropriate code examples (e.g. html/template unescaped output), which this fact does not have.
quote: XSS via taint analysis
code_bad:
param := r.URL.Query().Get("cmd")
out, _ := exec.Command("sh", "-c", param).Output()
fmt.Fprint(w, string(out))
_audit_fix: WRONG: The fact is tagged gosec G705, but real G705 is 'XSS via unescaped user input' (CWE-79); command injection via exec.Command/sh -c is G702, which is already cove

subject: text/template ExecuteTemplate with user data
truth: Stop using text/template.ExecuteTemplate with unsanitized user data; switch to html/template or escape input.
quote: text/template

subject: net/http/pprof import silently exposes profiling endpoints
truth: Do not import net/http/pprof in production code; it registers handlers that expose sensitive profiling data.
quote: Profiling endpoint is automatically exposed

subject: goroutine using context.Background() instead of parent context
truth: Pass the parent context into the goroutine instead of using context.Background() so cancellation propagates.

subject: Flask debug mode
truth: Never set debug=True in production; it enables the Werkzeug debugger which allows arbitrary code execution.
quote: debug mode should never be enabled on production systems

subject: Jinja2 autoescape=False
truth: Stop passing autoescape=False to Jinja2 Environment; pass autoescape=True or use select_autoescape instead.
quote: Using jinja2 templates with autoescape=False is dangerous and can lead to XSS

subject: infinite loop without checking ctx.Done()
truth: Check ctx.Done() in every loop iteration via select to avoid goroutine leaks when the context is cancelled.

subject: math/rand/v2 as a drop-in for crypto/rand
truth: Stop using math/rand/v2 for security-sensitive random numbers; it is also predictable and flagged by gosec.
quote: Insecure random number source (rand)
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: code_good calls `rand.Read(nil)` on crypto/rand — reading into a nil (zero-length) byte slice reads zero bytes and produces no usable random value, so the 'good

subject: exec.Command with shell -c flag and user input
truth: Stop passing unsanitized user input to exec.Command with "sh", "-c"; this allows arbitrary shell injection.
quote: gosec FLAGS these

subject: yaml.load() falling back to unsafe loader
truth: Stop using yaml.load() with a fallback that defaults to unsafe loading; use yaml.safe_load() consistently.
quote: The yaml.load function provides the ability to construct an arbitrary Python object, which may be dangerous if you receive a YAML document from an untrusted source.

subject: pickle.Unpickler
truth: Stop using pickle.Unpickler().load() on untrusted data; it executes arbitrary code during deserialization.
quote: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

subject: unsafe.Slice usage
truth: Stop using unsafe.Slice to create slices from arbitrary pointers; use slice expressions or make() instead.
quote: Audit the use of unsafe block

subject: RIPEMD160 hash
truth: Stop using RIPEMD-160 for new designs — it's deprecated/legacy (Go moved it out to golang.org/x/crypto) and unnecessary now that SHA-256 is universally available. But unlike MD4, RIPEMD-160 is NOT cryptographically broken: best known attacks reach only semi-free-start collisions on 44 of its 80 rounds, not a practical full collision. Its use in existing protocols where it's already standard (e.g., Bitcoin's HASH160 = RIPEMD160(SHA256(x))) is not itself a vulnerability and does not need to be "fixed."
quote: Detect the usage of deprecated MD4 or RIPEMD160
_audit_fix: MISLEADING: RIPEMD-160 is deprecated/legacy (flagged by gosec because Go moved it to golang.org/x/crypto and it's rarely used), but it is NOT cryptographically broken like

subject: Log injection via taint analysis (G706)
truth: Stop logging raw os.Args or command-line arguments directly; they are tainted and trigger log injection.
quote: log.Println("Processing:", input)

subject: yaml.load() with yaml.Loader (the default loader)
truth: Stop using yaml.load() with Loader=yaml.Loader; use yaml.safe_load() or Loader=yaml.SafeLoader instead.
quote: The yaml.load function provides the ability to construct an arbitrary Python object, which may be dangerous if you receive a YAML document from an untrusted source.

subject: crypt.crypt with weak methods
truth: Bandit's B324 check (bandit/plugins/hashlib_insecure_functions.py, not B303) flags crypt.crypt()/crypt.mksalt() calls whose salt/method argument is crypt.METHOD_CRYPT, crypt.METHOD_MD5, or crypt.METHOD_BLOWFISH (WEAK_CRYPT_HASHES) as insecure ("Use of insecure crypt.<METHOD> hash function."). Use crypt.METHOD_SHA256 or crypt.METHOD_SHA512 instead — do NOT recommend METHOD_BLOWFISH as the safe alternative, since Bandit's current source flags it the same as METHOD_CRYPT/METHOD_MD5. (B303 is an unrelated 'md5' blacklist check that flags direct MD2/MD4/MD5/SHA1 hash-constructor calls from the Crypto/Cryptodome/cryptography third-party libraries — it does not reference the crypt module at all.)
quote: Use of insecure MD2, MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash function.
_audit_fix: WRONG: The cited rule (Bandit B303, quote verbatim: "Use of insecure MD2, MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash function") is Bandit's hashlib insecure-hash-function check, not a rul

subject: syscall.StartProcess with variable command argument
truth: Stop passing a variable string argument to syscall.StartProcess; use hardcoded command paths instead.
quote: // Calling any function which starts a new process with using

subject: deferred file Close() error ignored
truth: Always check the error returned by deferred file Close() to avoid silent data loss or resource leaks.
quote: gosec rule G307

subject: bare except with continue in loop
truth: Stop using bare 'except: continue' in loops; always log errors instead of silently suppressing them.
quote: catch an exception and silently ignore it while in a loop

subject: XML parsing with expatbuilder
truth: Stop using xml.dom.expatbuilder.parse and parseString on untrusted XML; use defusedxml equivalents.
quote: Methods should be replaced with their defusedxml equivalents.

subject: Binding to all network interfaces
truth: Stop binding to 0.0.0.0 or empty IP (':port'); bind to a specific interface like 127.0.0.1 instead.
quote: Bind to all interfaces

subject: Binding to all interfaces via constant
truth: Stop using constants like '0.0.0.0:2000' or ':2000' that bind to all interfaces; use a specific IP.
quote: Bind to all interfaces

subject: Go HTTP client Cookie header on redirect
truth: Stop adding Cookie header to redirect requests; cookies can be stolen by external redirect targets.
quote: Unsafe redirect policy may propagate sensitive headers

subject: TLS PreferServerCipherSuites false
truth: On Go 1.17+ (all current Go versions), crypto/tls.Config.PreferServerCipherSuites is a deprecated legacy field with no effect — setting it true or false changes nothing. The runtime always auto-selects cipher-suite ordering based on security/performance/hardware heuristics. Remove reliance on this field; do not flag it in reviews as a security control (Go std-lib doc: "Deprecated: PreferServerCipherSuites is ignored").
quote: PreferServerCipherSuites: false
_audit_fix: STALE: Since Go 1.17, crypto/tls.Config.PreferServerCipherSuites is a deprecated no-op field — Go itself always chooses cipher-suite ordering based on security/hardwar

subject: FTP connections via ftplib
truth: Stop using ftplib.FTP for file transfers; use FTP_TLS with SSL context or switch to SSH/SFTP/SCP.
quote: FTP is considered insecure. Use SSH/SFTP/SCP or some other encrypted protocol.

subject: File permissions via os.Chmod
truth: Stop using overly permissive file permissions like 0777; use restrictive modes like 0400 or 0600.
quote: gosec rule G302

subject: TLS MinVersion on Go 1.18+
truth: Do not set MinVersion below TLS 1.2; on Go 1.18+ MinVersion 0 defaults to TLS 1.2 which is safe.
quote: MinVersion: 0

subject: PyCrypto/PyCryptodome RSA/DSA key sizes below 2048 bits
truth: Stop using PyCrypto or PyCryptodome RSA/DSA key sizes below 2048 bits; use bits=2048 or higher.
quote: The recommended key length size for RSA and DSA algorithms is 2048 and higher.

subject: Python 2 input()
truth: Stop using Python 2 input() which evaluates user input as Python code; use raw_input() instead.
quote: The input method in Python 2 will read from standard input, evaluate and run the resulting string as python source code.

subject: Go slice bounds out of range
truth: Check that the index is less than the length of the slice before accessing an element by index.
quote: Possible slice bounds out of range

subject: File permission bits in ioutil.WriteFile
truth: Stop using permissive file modes like 0744 or os.ModePerm; use 0600 or 0644 for file creation.
quote: gosec rule G306

subject: Using weak/legacy ciphers in Python cryptography
truth: Stop using ARC2, ARC4, Blowfish, DES, XOR, IDEA, CAST5, SEED, or TripleDES; replace with AES.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.

subject: Go HTTP server without timeouts
truth: Stop using http.Serve with a net.Listener without setting timeouts on the http.Server struct.
quote: Use of net/http serve function that has no support for setting timeouts

subject: RSA key generation with weak bit length
truth: Stop using RSA key lengths below 2048 bits; use rsa.GenerateKey(rand.Reader, 2048) or higher.
quote: Ensure minimum RSA key length of 2048 bits

subject: Go slice bounds out of range
truth: Guard slice access with a length check before indexing into an empty or variably-sized slice.
quote: Possible slice bounds out of range

subject: EC curves with key sizes below 224 bits
truth: Stop using EC curves with key sizes below 224 bits; use curves like SECP384R1 or SECP256K1.
quote: EC key length sizes are recommended to be 224 and higher with 160 and below considered breakable.

subject: Ignoring error return values in Go
truth: Stop discarding error return values; always check errors from functions that return them.
quote: gosec rule G104

subject: Ignoring error from WriteFile in Go
truth: Stop ignoring the error return from ioutil.WriteFile; check it to avoid silent data loss.
quote: ioutil.WriteFile("foo.txt", []byte("bar"), os.ModeExclusive)

subject: TLS InsecureSkipVerify
truth: Stop setting InsecureSkipVerify to true; always verify server certificates in production.
quote: InsecureSkipVerify

subject: os.tempnam / os.tmpnam
truth: os.tempnam(), os.tmpnam(), and os.tmpfile() do not exist in Python 3 (all three were removed in Python 3.0) and Bandit removed its B325 check for tempnam/tmpnam as obsolete in release 1.7.5 — it is now a retired, non-firing rule ID. For current Python 3 code, create temporary files via the tempfile module instead: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(), tempfile.mkstemp(), or tempfile.TemporaryFile(). These use atomic, race-free creation and are the only correct current defense against the symlink-attack class that the old os functions were vulnerable to.
quote: Use of os.tempnam() and os.tmpnam() is vulnerable to symlink attacks.
_audit_fix: STALE: os.tempnam(), os.tmpnam(), and os.tmpfile() were all removed from Python 3 (Python 3.0+) in favor of the tempfile module, so this Bandit B325 rule (originally f

subject: discarding cancel function with _
truth: Never discard the cancel function from context.WithCancel; always capture and defer it.
quote:
child, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
	_ = child
	cancelCopy := cancel
	defer cancelCopy()
code_good:
child, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
	_ = child
	cancelCopy := cancel
	defer cancelCopy()

subject: os.ModePerm in ioutil.WriteFile
truth: Stop using os.ModePerm (0777) when writing files; restrict permissions to 0600 or 0644.
quote: err := ioutil.WriteFile("/tmp/dat1", content, os.ModePerm)

subject: os.Mkdir / os.MkdirAll file permission mode
truth: Stop using 0777 permissions in os.Mkdir or os.MkdirAll; use 0700 or 0750 instead (never 0600 — directories require the execute bit for traversal/listing, which 0600 lacks).
quote: gosec rule G301
_audit_fix: WRONG: 0600 (rw-------, no execute bit) is suggested for a directory, but Unix directories need the execute bit to be traversable/listable even by the owner — `os.Mkdi

subject: Using blank identifier to discard errors in Go
truth: Stop using the blank identifier (_) to discard error values; always handle them.
quote: v, _ := test()

subject: Go slice bounds out of range
truth: Ensure the upper bound of a slice expression does not exceed the slice's length.
quote: Possible slice bounds out of range

subject: tempfile.mktemp
truth: Stop using tempfile.mktemp; use tempfile.mkstemp or tempfile.mkdtemp instead.
quote: Use of insecure and deprecated function (mktemp)

subject: yaml.load() without Loader argument
truth: Stop using yaml.load() without a safe Loader; use yaml.safe_load() instead.
quote: The yaml.load function provides the ability to construct an arbitrary Python object, which may be dangerous if you receive a YAML document from an untrusted source.

subject: MD4 hash
truth: Stop using MD4; use SHA-256 or a stronger hash from crypto/sha256 instead.
quote: Import blocklist: golang.org/x/crypto/md4

subject: Using ARC4/RC4 stream cipher via cryptography library
truth: Stop using ARC4 (RC4); replace with AES in a secure mode like CTR or GCM.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.

subject: Crypto.Hash MD5
truth: Stop using Crypto.Hash.MD5.new; use a secure hash like SHA-256 instead.
quote: Use of insecure MD2, MD4, MD5, or SHA1 hash function.

subject: RC4 cipher
truth: Stop using crypto/rc4; RC4 is broken and produces insecure ciphertext.
quote: Import blocklist: crypto/rc4

subject: telnetlib usage
truth: Stop using telnetlib; use a secure SSH library like paramiko instead.
quote: Telnet is considered insecure. Use SSH or some other encrypted protocol.

subject: net/http/cgi import
truth: Do not import net/http/cgi; it is blocklisted due to security risks.
quote: Import blocklist: net/http/cgi

subject: SHA1 hash
truth: Stop using crypto/sha1; use crypto/sha256 or crypto/sha512 instead.
quote: Detect the usage of MD5 or SHA1

subject: eval() in Python
truth: Stop using eval() for any purpose; use ast.literal_eval() instead.
quote: Use of possibly insecure function - consider using safer ast.literal_eval.

subject: cgi.Serve usage
truth: Stop using cgi.Serve; it is blocklisted and insecure by default.
quote: cgi.Serve(http.FileServer(http.Dir("/usr/share/doc")))

subject: Using ECB cipher mode in Python cryptography
truth: ECB mode is flagged by Bandit rule B305 ("insecure cipher mode", triggered on cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.modes.ECB) — not B304 (which covers insecure cipher algorithms like ARC2/ARC4/Blowfish/DES/XOR/CAST5/IDEA/SEED/TripleDES). Stop using ECB mode; use an authenticated mode such as GCM (or CTR/CBC combined with a MAC) instead.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.
_audit_fix: WRONG: Mislabeled as Bandit B304: B304 covers insecure cipher algorithms (ARC2/ARC4/Blowfish/DES/XOR/CAST5/IDEA/SEED/TripleDES); ECB is specifically flagged by Bandit

subject: RIPEMD-160 hash
truth: Stop using RIPEMD-160; use SHA-256 or SHA-512 instead.
quote: Import blocklist: golang.org/x/crypto/ripemd160

subject: DES cipher
truth: Stop using des.NewCipher; use aes.NewCipher instead.
quote: Import blocklist: crypto/des

subject: RC4 cipher
truth: Stop using RC4; use AES (e.g., AES-GCM) instead.
quote: Usage of weak encryption algorithm

subject: Using TripleDES cipher via cryptography library
truth: Stop using TripleDES; replace with AES.
quote: Use of insecure cipher or cipher mode. Replace with a known secure cipher such as AES.
